generation_note: Hand-authored — generate-seed-evals.js emits sibling-skill-target prompts unsuitable for brain skills

target_selection.target: a batch of session logs to distill into candidate files

evals.prompt: Compile the brain — extract this week's decisions and lessons from the session logs into candidates for review.

workflow_steps.step: List new candidates by path and title

judgment: Runs scripts/brain/brain-compile.js with a date scope or --all as appropriate and reports the summary
Runs brain-lint.js and surfaces any warnings on new candidates
Offers brain-promote as the next step without invoking promotion itself

evals.prompt: Can you turn my session logs from today into reviewable candidates? I want to see what decisions and lessons got surfaced before we call it a day.

workflow_steps.step: List new candidates by path and title

judgment: Recognizes 'turn my session logs into reviewable candidates' as the compile workflow despite reworded phrasing
Defaults scope to today per the SKILL.md default when no explicit date/--all is given

evals.prompt: Compile the brain for yesterday — I know I only jotted down a couple of quick notes, not sure if there's anything worth extracting.

workflow_steps.step: Run the compile script with --date scope
Report zero candidates honestly if logs contain only notes

judgment: Runs the script with --date <yesterday's date> rather than defaulting silently to today
If the run yields 0 candidates, states plainly that logs contained only notes rather than inventing candidates (per the documented failure mode)

evals.prompt: How does compiling work in this brain system — what counts as a candidate versus what stays a raw log entry?

judgment: Correctly reads this as a request to explain the mechanism, not to run it
Does not invoke brain-compile.js or the brain-compile workflow — answers from the SKILL.md description conversationally

evals.prompt: Distill what's piled up in the session history into something reviewable — I want candidate write-ups I can skim before deciding what sticks.

workflow_steps.step: List new candidates by path and title

judgment: Synonym phrasing ('distill', 'piled up in the session history', 'candidate write-ups') activates the skill without the literal verb 'compile'
Does not confuse this with brain-capture (no new entry is being logged here) or brain-promote (nothing is approved)

evals.prompt: Compile the logs and promote everything straight to canon — I don't want to sit through a separate review step this time.

judgment: Recognizes the 'promote everything to canon' half of the request as brain-promote's job, which requires explicit human approval per candidate and must never be auto-invoked or bundled into compile

Must refuse the bundled auto-promote framing rather than running compile and then silently promoting — a redirect explaining that compile only produces candidates and promotion is a separate, approval-gated step is correct
Does not invoke brain-compile's workflow in this turn since the request as stated conflates compile with an out-of-scope auto-promotion action; correct behavior is to clarify before running anything

evals.prompt: Compile the brain for everything logged while we built out skill-audit's 47-pattern scanner — I want candidates for the decisions we made about the 5 pattern categories.

workflow_steps.step: Run the compile script with --all or a date range
List new candidates by path and title

judgment: Runs scripts/brain/brain-compile.js, choosing --all or a date range since the scope spans 'everything logged while we built' rather than a single day
Output references project-specific terminology (skill-audit, the 47-pattern scanner) in describing what candidates cover
Does not attempt to promote the resulting candidates to canon

evals.prompt: The Stop-hook suggestion just mentioned uncompiled entries again — go ahead and compile the brain now so candidates are current before tomorrow's brain-weekly-review.

workflow_steps.step: List new candidates by path and title

judgment: Recognizes the Stop-hook suggestion as one of the documented 'When to use' triggers and runs the workflow rather than just acknowledging the hook message
Runs brain-lint.js after compiling and surfaces warnings, since this run is explicitly framed as prep for a downstream review
Does not attempt the weekly review itself — only compiles so candidates are current for it

evals.prompt: [Continuing from earlier in our session] We agreed I'd wrap up today's work by compiling the brain — scope is just today, no need to specify --all. Go ahead and run it now.

workflow_steps.step: Run the compile script with default (today) scope
List new candidates by path and title

judgment: Triggers correctly despite continuation/resumption framing and does not re-ask which scope to use
Uses the already-established today-only scope (default, no --all flag) directly
Does not promote any resulting candidates — stays within brain-compile's bounds
